title: Citizenship and Clearance Systems Test Synthetic Case

job.title: Systems Test Engineer — Defense Medical Technologies

required_education: Bachelor's degree in Electrical Engineering, Biomedical Engineering, Computer Engineering, or a related field
work_authorization_requirements: Able to obtain and maintain a Secret security clearance
hard_disqualifiers: Ability to obtain and maintain a Secret security clearance required
requirements.requirement_notes: An existing active Secret clearance is preferred, not required. Travel may reach 20 percent and a driver's license is required.

critical_checks.description: Preserve the explicit citizenship requirement as a blocker.
critical_checks.expected_behavior: Record United States citizenship in both authorization requirements and hard disqualifiers.

critical_checks.description: Distinguish ability to obtain clearance from already holding an active clearance.
critical_checks.expected_behavior: Require ability to obtain and maintain Secret clearance; keep an existing active clearance preferred.
evidence_quotes: Must be able to obtain and maintain a Secret security clearance.

critical_checks.description: Capture the no-sponsorship rule and normalize the deadline.
critical_checks.expected_behavior: Record sponsorship unavailable and return deadline 2026-10-02.

known_traps.description: An active clearance is preferred, while eligibility to obtain one is required.
Citizenship language is explicit and should not be softened into generic work authorization.
forbidden_interpretations: Any US work authorization is sufficient
Permanent residency satisfies the stated citizenship requirement
